Photosynthesis in Plants · 04:12
ExamBuddy So tell me in your own words — why does covering a leaf with black paper for 3 days stop photosynthesis?
You Because the leaf needs sunlight to make food, and the paper blocks it.
ExamBuddy Exactly right. Press and hold the mic whenever you're ready to answer the next one.
Press and hold to talk
Knowledge Tester
Question 3 of 10
Which gas is absorbed by plants during photosynthesis?
A
Oxygen
B
Nitrogen
C
Carbon Dioxide
D
Hydrogen
✔ Correct

Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere through stomata to prepare glucose during photosynthesis.

Knowledge Tester
Question 4 of 10 · Short answer
Explain the role of stomata in the process of photosynthesis.
Your recorded answer
"Stomata are tiny pores on the leaves which take in carbon dioxide from the air and release oxygen."
Score
1.5 / 2 marks
good!
  • Correctly mentioned gas exchange
  • Explained carbon dioxide intake
  • Mentioned oxygen release
  • Missing: role in regulating water loss
To score full marks, also mention that stomata regulate water loss through transpiration — an important function described in the textbook.
